item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Pretty cool story, definitely deserves an upvote, but I feel DNA testing could be run on the flesh. Not testing it does add to the mystery though, +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: is there some sort of joke or reference i'm not getting? am i supposed to know who Miriam Wells is? there seem to be other articles on this site (at least one found via Google search) that reference her, but nothing is indicated in this article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can totally see this article being interesting and/or scary for other people, but in my opinion its too simple. Also reminds me of some typical creepypastas. Novote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This reads like a Series I article.
I personally would change the last line from "The source of this is unknown." to "The source of this has not been determined." or something like that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Short and sweet, yet amazing. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I ended up downvoting this. It's certainly well written and I don't think it needs to be longer, but I've heard stories that sound just like this one. The article doesn't offer anything particularly new to the classic narrative (aside from details) so I didn't think this qualified for my upvote.

item.title: A bit too short but good
item.content:encoded:
I think the Foundation would try to understand the decomposed flesh anomaly, and not just say that "the origin is unknown".

Nevertheless, it's still a good article.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ghost stories have to work hard to make me care. This one doesn't.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel the same way. It's good, but the straightforwardness doesn't interest me enough to upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Added a fictional town. This may become important in later instalments.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This reminds me of a number of series I articles, but it hits a spot where I'm not sure there's actually enough here, while being unable to suggest anything to add.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No-vote. I've never been of the opinion that "too short" warrants a downvote when everything else about an article is well-done, but this doesn't intrigue me either.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Will explore this in later tales, but tl;dr: she disappoofs from your car when you come out.

item.title: Well written post on familiar ground
item.content:encoded:
It's too well done to down vote, but it is too familiar territory to up vote.

Should we consider whether consider where there are other less deserving entries?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In a minority of observed cases, subjects report being able to convince the woman to join them in their vehicle. All such subjects later find a piece of heavily scorched and decomposed human flesh in the storage compartment of their vehicle. The source of this is unknown.

Do they have memory loss, or something? The article never mentions what the subjects' experience with her is, though I assume she's just pulp and mulch from centuries of hellfire, and that's what's trying to escape.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Tentative upvote from me given that Aiden fixed up the use of "subject" and I'm all for short and sweet SCP articles.

I'm not totally in love with the utter simplicity since I agree with sirpudding that it's a pretty straightforward ghost story, but the sense of vague unsettling-ness stuck with me (since being dehydrated is absolutely terrible, and I hate long trips in cars) so that's a +1 for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm not sure there's quite enough for me in this regardless, but this:

the New South Wales region of the outback

sealed my downvote. That's approximately 500,000 km2 of country you're talking about - the Foundation needs to be a little more precise than that!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It was specifically intended to be a hyper-ascetic exercise in cutting things down to the bare bones, yeah. Glad to see it worked!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That was my feeling on it: so much so that I downvoted and then cancelled.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The brevity of this article is sooo refreshing. It doesn't need to be any longer.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I kinda like the concept but I don't really feel there's enough to this, I really think you should expand and develop this somehow. -1 for now

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This seems like a good, but otherwise straightforward ghost story, and greatly resembles a mix of three of four local stories here (the dimension portal on Sanjon Road, the phantom hitchhiker on Central, and Char-man; but I live in haunted Ventura County so I can probably do this with any sort of ghost story at all). I think I will no-vote on that basis.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Aiden, you are so late to this party I've already made the late to the party joke.

In all seriousness, I feel like this does use its brevity well, and despite its short length does a solid job getting its narrative across. (Poor Officer Wells)

Additionally, the article strikes me as a combination of a lot of the fun aspects of SCP-1337 and SCP-973, without really coming across as a rip off of ether.

+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I still think it could be longer, but I don't think it needs to be. +1

item.title: woo
item.content:encoded:
Image by Murray Foubister available under this license

215 words in its final form, which means it's eligible for the bonus contest points.

Thanks to LordStonefish, halothane, This Is War, Jacob Conwell and Roget for crit. Not sure if this'll sink or swim, but we'll see how it does in the Short Works Contest.

ETA: 218 after edits.
